channel.title: forensics on Analyst
channel.description: Recent content in forensics on Analyst

item.title: Incident Response
item.description: Intro CSIRT - Computer Security Incident Response Team Data Breach is not the same as Security Breach.

item.title: Slacking Insider Threats by Magnet Forensics
item.description: These are my course notes from Slacking on insider threat by Magnet Forensics. Thank you, guys, for sharing!

item.title: File System Forensics
item.description: My notes of several forensics books. Including but not limited to “File System Forensic Analysis”.

item.title: Memory Forensics
item.description: This is my course notes from SDF series, Memory Forensics, Part 1. Intro Installed volatile on mac:

item.title: Prefetch Forensics
item.description: This is my course notes from Prefetch forensics series. Tools WinPrefetchView To view pretech files (decompressed as well) in GUI.

item.title: Dfir Intro
item.description: This is my lecture notes of DFIR intro course. As I learn more that was provided in the course, I’ll add a reference and data.

item.title: Ios Reverse
item.description: During forensic analysis it’s not too rare to run into some suspicious application. In this article I’m going to learn to reverse engineer iOS applications.

item.title: Mobile Forensic Analysis Introduction
item.description: Tool leveling pyramid The methods are placed in the order from the easiest to the hardest and most time-consuming:

item.title: Phases
item.description: This article is about the phases of mobile forensics.

item.title: Ios Screen Time
item.description: Overview Address book 📖 Call history ☎️ SMS 💬 MMS 💬 🌄 Email ✉️ Web browser history Photos 📷 Videos 📹 Music 🎵 Documents 📃 Calendar 📆 Network communication 🌐 Maps 🗺️ Social networking 🦜 Deleted data 🗑️ https://www.

item.title: Android Cheatsheet
item.description: This cheasheet is to ease the flow of either penetration testing analysis or forensics investigation. Fill in the form below to speed up command line tools usage.

item.title: iOS Cheatsheet
item.description: This cheasheet is to ease the flow of either penetration testing analysis or forensics investigation. Fill in the form below to speed up command line tools usage.

item.title: Mac Setup Script
item.description: # oh my zsh bash themes sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)" # sh -c "$(fetch -o - https://raw.

item.title: Ios Loggs
item.description: About iOS logs here. Syslog According to Apple docs, NSLog now doesn’t write to syslog, therefore I didn’t find a syslog.

item.title: Lab Setup
item.description: Analysing Traffic Dshell Installing sudo python3 -m pip install [tar_file] # option 1 pip install dshell # option 2.

item.title: BTFM
item.description: Dshell # MacOSX path to directory with pyenv (example): /Users/[user_name]/.pyenv/versions/3.8.5/envs/python3/lib/python3.8/site-packages/dshell/ decode -l # list decoders decode -d [decoder_name] # decoder info # who talked to whom decode -d ip [capture_file_name].

item.title: Laboratory Setup
item.description: This article is a walkthrough of setting up the lab for pentesting iOS applications and forensic analysis of iDevices and Android devices (the main purpose of a tool, installation steps and possible issues along the way).

item.title: Acquisition Methods
item.description: In this article I’m studying different acquisition methods, tool available for each, pros and cons of each tool as well.

item.title: Live Acquisition Steps
item.description: Prerequisites USB with portable exes? Live USB Remote? RAM acquisition tools Video RAM Acquisition Steps RAM Acquisition is the first priority since it’s the volatile memory.

item.title: Offline Acquisition Steps
item.description: Prerequisites Live USB/CD? Process Connect external HDD or SSD Lauch some forensic distribution (for example, Paladin).

item.title: BTFM and RTFM iOS
item.description: This is a combination of BTFM and RTFM (Blue Team Field Manual and Red Team Field Manual) for iOS devices and applications.

item.title: Forensics vs Pentest
item.description: Forensics vs Penetration testing These two fields are closely related, for you have to acquire the same knowledge but you are just using it differently.

item.title: Attacking Android
item.description: Attacking device For this attack there are several types of attacker: • MiM, i.e. a sniffer.

item.title: BTFM and RTFM Android
item.description: This is a combination of BTFM and RTFM (Blue Team Field Manual and Red Team Field Manual) for Android devices and applications.

item.title: iOS Jailbreaking
item.description: Types of jailbreaking Tethered jailbreaks don’t persist through reboots, so re-applying jailbreaks requires the device to be connected (tethered) to a computer during every reboot.
